summary:
\boxed{Multiple pregnancies are identified as high-risk infants because they involve higher likelihoods of various complications during delivery and post-birth care needs.}

The other factors do not independently elevate an individual baby’s or overall pregnancy complication rate significantly enough for classification without further specific details indicating otherwise. Thus focusing specifically addresses why option D stands out among given choices based solely upon typical medical criteria used by clinicians when assessing neonatal health outcomes at birth stage considerations alone.
